concerns.text: Task T010 is marked `[P]` but depends on the download step (T010 itself) to provide data for extraction (T011); it cannot safely run in parallel with T011 or T012.
Task T011 is marked `[P]` yet requires the output of T010 (downloaded archives). Parallel execution would cause missing inputs.
Task T012 is marked `[P]` but consumes the extracted source files produced by T011; it must run after T011.

Task T019 `[P]` (train primary model) depends on the processed dataset created by US1 (T015). It cannot start before US1 completes, contradicting the claim that US2 can run in parallel after foundation.
Task T020 `[P]` (train alternative model) also depends on the US1 dataset; parallel execution before US1 finishes violates data‑flow ordering.
Task T028 `[P]` (generate PDPs) requires the importance rankings from T021, which are produced after model training; it is not parallel‑safe.
Task T035 `[P]` (cache metric results) depends on metric extraction (T012). Caching cannot be performed before metrics exist.
Task T036 `[P]` (checksum verification for downloads) depends on the download script (T010). It must run after T010, not in parallel.
Task T037 `[P]` (run full test suite) should execute after all implementation tasks are complete; marking it `[P]` suggests it could run concurrently, which would miss many required artifacts.
User Story 2 (Phase 4) is described as runnable in parallel with US1, yet it consumes the dataset produced by US1. This violates the producer‑before‑consumer rule.
User Story 3 (Phase 5) consumes model artifacts from US2 and the dataset from US1, but the plan states it can start in parallel after foundation. This is an ordering violation.
Task T031 is marked `[P]` but updates the README with usage instructions that reference artifacts produced later (e.g., model files). It should run after those artifacts exist.
Task T032 `[P]` adds documentation for pipeline stages that may reference files not yet created (e.g., `code/data/split_dataset.py`). Documentation should be generated after those files exist.
